LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MATTHEW C. KRAMER, IN HIS REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY AS WARDEN OF FOLSOM STATE PRISON, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH IS WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF OHSA. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH ALLEGE COMMON LAW CLAIMS WHERE AN ALLEGED DUTY IS BREACHED. GIVEN THE RELIEF SOUGHT, PLAINTIFF DID NOT NEED TO FILE A TORT CLAIM - GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 814. IN ADDITION, THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFFS REQUEST TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY. DEFENDANT HAS 20 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 9956

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas And Notices Of Deposition Of Drs. Cafarella, Desai, Getman, And Sobelman
Off Calendar Oct-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jennifer Stoughton, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: California Correctional Peace Officer's Association
Dana Brown, Esq., State of California, Labor Relations Counsel and
Jenny L. Esquivel, Esq., Department of Personnel Administration, for Defendant and opposing party: Matthew C. Kramer, State of Californis, California Department of Corrections, John Chiang and Division of Occupational Safety and Health

After hearing, Plaintiff California Correctional Peace Officer Association's Motion to quash or limit deposition subpoenas and notice of deposition of Drs. Cafarella, Desai, Getman, and Sobelman is Granted in Part. Deposition subpoenas and testimony shall be limited to records and testimony of symptoms, diagnosis and sources of Plaintiff's MRSA infections, Staph infection or recurrences of MRSA or Stap infections from January 1, 2006 to the present. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MATTHEW C. KRAMER, in his representative capacity as Warden of Folsom State Prison, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, and CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: James W. Henderson, Jr., Esq. & Jennifer S. Stoughton, Esq. of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP 989-5900 for pltfs & opposing parties; James Casey Spurling, State of California, Asst Chief Counsel, 916/324-0512 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH IS WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF OHSA. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH ALLEGE COMMON LAW CLAIMS WHERE AN ALLEGED DUTY IS BREACHED. GIVEN THE RELIEF SOUGHT, PLAINTIFF DID NOT NEED TO FILE A TORT CLAIM - GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 814. In addition, the Court granted plaintiffs request to lift discovery stay. Defendant has 20 days to answer. Plaintiff to prepare proposed form of order. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 9956

Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Order To Compel Production Of Documents Indentified By Peter Damaiano During His Deposition On Oct-10-07
Off Calendar Feb-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Jennifer Stoughton, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: California Correctional Peace Officers' Association, Gary Benson, Pam Coleman, Chris Pearson and David White
James C. Spurling, Assistant Chief Counsel, for defendant and opposing party: Matthew C. Kramer, State of California and California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION. Plaintiff California Correctional Peace Officers's motion to compel production of documents is granted in part. Defendant is ordered to produce the lists of individuals specified in the deposition of Peter Damiano. Defendant may redact the personal identifying information of the inmates and may alternatively identity said individuals by their initials or the last three digits of the prisoner identification number.
MODIFICATION: Compliance by February 29, 2008. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MATTHEW KRAMER, WARDEN OF FOLSOM STATE PRISON, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: GREGG ADAM, ESQUIRE ( CARROLL BURDICK, 415.743.2534) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES C. SPURLING, ASSISTANT CHIEF COUNSEL (STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPT. OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION, LEGAL DIVISION, 916.324.0512) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE CAL/O.S.H.A. HAS EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OVER THE CLAIMS AND IS IN THE PROCESS OF ADJUDICATING THE CLAIMS. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN THIRTY DAYS AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD A CLAIM LIMITED TO THE ALLEGATIONS SUMMARIZED IN PARAGRAPH 74. THE COURT LACKS THE AUTHORITY UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 11106 TO DIRECT CAL/O.S.H.A. NOT TO STAY ENFORCEMENT OF THE OCTOBER 30, 2007 CITATIONS. COURT, AT THE REQUEST OF COUNSEL, ISSUES A STAY OF DISCOVERY, EXCEPT THE DEFENDANTS HAVE A RIGHT TO FILE MOTIONS TO COMPEL DISCOVERY; IF GRANTED, WOULD NOT BE EFFECTUATED UNTIL AFTER THE DISCOVERY STAY IS LIFTED. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN CHIANG'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.
APPEARANCES: GREGG ADAM, ESQUIRE (CARROLL BURDICK, 415.743.2534) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES C. SPURLING, ASSISTANT CHIEF COUNSEL (STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPT. OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION, LEGAL DIVISION, 916.324.0512) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: COUNSEL SUBMIT TO THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE ANY WRONGFUL CONDUCT ON THE PART OF THE CONTROLLER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
